entities.name: Fitbit API
entities.context: REST API used to fetch daily health metrics (sleep, HR, weight) via batch endpoints

entities.name: sync_daily_metrics
entities.context: Python function that syncs sleep, resting HR, and weight data over a date range

entities.name: Sleep Tracking
entities.context: Identified as the most important daily metric, prioritized first in sync order

entities.name: Resting Heart Rate
entities.context: Daily resting HR metric synced from Fitbit

entities.name: Weight Tracking
entities.context: Daily weight metric synced from Fitbit

entities.name: HRV
entities.context: Heart Rate Variability — per-day endpoint removed to stay within rate limits

entities.name: SpO2
entities.context: Blood oxygen saturation — per-day endpoint removed to stay within rate limits

entities.name: Skin Temperature
entities.context: Per-day endpoint removed to stay within rate limits

entities.name: Stress Score
entities.context: Per-day endpoint removed to stay within rate limits

entities.name: Rate Limiting
entities.context: API call constraint that drove design decision to use only batch endpoints

entities.name: workout_tracker
entities.context: External tracker object passed into sync function, likely coordinates with workout data

entities.name: Sleep Score
entities.context: Composite sleep quality score extracted from log or levels summary

entities.name: Batch Endpoints
entities.context: API pattern using date-range queries to minimize individual API calls

relationships.context: Calls Fitbit REST endpoints to fetch sleep, HR, and weight data
Sleep is synced first as the most important metric
Resting HR synced as part of daily metrics
Weight synced as part of daily metrics
Uses only batch date-range endpoints to minimize API calls
HRV per-day calls removed due to rate limit constraints
SpO2 per-day calls removed due to rate limit constraints
Skin temp per-day calls removed due to rate limit constraints
Stress score per-day calls removed due to rate limit constraints
Rate limits drove architectural decision to prefer batch endpoints
Sleep score is a quality metric extracted from each sleep log
Receives workout_tracker as parameter, likely for cross-referencing health data
